zh.lifeSummary: 當Bishop Neal "Nigel" of Ely Le Poer於1100年出生在英國坎布利其爾埃利時，他的父親Bishop Humphrey of Salisbury Le Poer30歲，母親Ada Le Poer15歲。他和Maud Mistress of Neil Nigel Ramsbury至少有1個兒子。他去世於1169年5月30日在英國英格蘭坎布利其爾埃利享年69歲。

zh-hans.Ely, Cambridgeshire, , England: 英国英格兰坎布利其爾埃利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Bishop Neal "Nigel" of Ely Le Poer于1100年出生在英国坎布利其爾埃利时，他的父亲Bishop Humphrey of Salisbury Le Poer30岁，母亲Ada Le Poer15岁。他和Maud Mistress of Neil Nigel Ramsbury至少有1个儿子。他去世于1169年5月30日在英国英格兰坎布利其爾埃利享年69岁。

nameMeanings.name: Ely
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Ely, a cathedral city on an island in the fens north of Cambridge, so named from Old English ǣl ‘eel’ + gē ‘district’.
English, French, and Breton (Finistère): from the Middle English, Old French personal name Elie, Hely (Elijah in the King James Bible); see Ellis and Elias .
English: variant of   Healey  .

variants.name: Ellis
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Elis, an Old French vernacular form of Elias, the Latin and New Testament Greek form of Hebrew Eliyahu; see Elijah and compare Elias , Lias , Ely .
English: possibly in some instances from the Middle English female personal name Elice, a pet form of Elizabeth .

Welsh: from Elis, a shortened form of the Welsh personal name Elisse (earlier Elisedd, a derivative of elus ‘kindly, benevolent’). As usual in Welsh, the stress in Elisse is on the penultimate syllable -li-, which shifts to initial El- when the name is shortened to Elis. It later became confused with Ellis in 1 above.

variants.name: Iley
meanings: English (Durham): habitational name from Brent Eleigh or Monks Eleigh in Suffolk, or perhaps from Illey in Worcestershire. The Suffolk placename derives from an Old English personal name Illa (genitive Illan) + Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’. The Worcestershire placename derives from the Old English personal name Hilla or Illa + lēah. Alternatively, the name may be a variant of Ely .
Perhaps an Americanized form of German   Ihle  .

variants.name: Elley
meanings: English: variant of   Ely   or   Ella  .

variants.name: Ealy
meanings: English: variant of   Ely  .

variants.name: Eley
meanings: English: variant of   Ely  .
German: from a vernacular form of the medieval personal name Eligius (see Eligio ), a Christian saint's name.

variants.name: Ealey
meanings: English: variant of   Ely  .

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Poer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Poor   (see   Power  ).

variants.name: Poor
meanings: English (southeastern): variant of   Power  . Compare   Poer  .
Hungarian (Poór): status name from pór ‘peasant, lower class’. Compare Por 2.
Iranian: variant of   Pour  .

nameMeanings.name: Neal
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of   Neil .

variants.name: Niall
meanings: Of disputed derivation; it may mean ‘cloud’, ‘passionate’, or perhaps ‘champion’. It was adopted by the Scandinavians in the form Njal and soon became very popular among them. From the Middle Ages onwards, this name was found mainly in Ireland, the Highlands of Scotland, and the English-Scottish Border region. However, it has been strongly revived among non-Gaelic speakers since the latter part of the 20th century.

variants.name: Neil
meanings: Anglicized form of the enduringly popular Gaelic name   Niall .  From the Middle Ages onwards, this name was found mainly in Ireland, the Highlands of Scotland, and the English-Scottish Border region. However, since the 20th century it has spread to enjoy considerable popularity in all parts of the English-speaking world, although in England it has recently been overtaken by the Gaelic form.

nameMeanings.name: "Nigel"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of the medieval name Nigellus, a Latinized version (ostensibly representing a diminutive of Latin niger ‘black’) of the vernacular Ni(h)el, i.e. Neil . Although it is frequently found in medieval records, this form was probably not used in everyday life before its revival by antiquarians such as Sir Walter Scott in the 19th century.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Bishop Neal "Nigel" of Ely Le Poer was born in 1100, in Ely, Cambridgeshire, England, his father, Bishop Humphrey of Salisbury Le Poer, was 30 and his mother, Ada Le Poer, was 15. He had at least 1 son with Maud Mistress of Neil Nigel Ramsbury. He died on 30 May 1169, in Ely, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 69.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Bishop Neal "Nigel" of Ely Le Poer est né en 1100, à Ely, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, son père, Bishop Humphrey of Salisbury Le Poer, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Ada Le Poer, avait 15 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Maud Mistress of Neil Nigel Ramsbury. Il est décédé le 30 mai 1169, à Ely, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 69 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Bishop Neal "Nigel" of Ely Le Poer 1100 in Ely, Cambridgeshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Bishop Humphrey of Salisbury Le Poer 30 und seine Mutter Ada Le Poer 15. Er hatte mit Maud Mistress of Neil Nigel Ramsbury mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 30. Mai 1169 im Alter von 69 Jahren in Ely, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Bishop Neal "Nigel" of Ely Le Poer è nato il nel 1100 nel Ely, Cambridgeshire, England, il suo padre, Bishop Humphrey of Salisbury Le Poer, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Ada Le Poer, aveva 15 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Maud Mistress of Neil Nigel Ramsbury. Morì il 30 maggio 1169, nel Ely, Cambridgeshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 69 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Bishop Neal "Nigel" of Ely Le Poerが1100年にイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアエリーで生まれた時，父Bishop Humphrey of Salisbury Le Poerは30歳，母Ada Le Poerは15歳であった。彼はMaud Mistress of Neil Nigel Ramsburyとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1169年5月30日にイギリスイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアエリーで69歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Bishop Neal "Nigel" of Ely Le Poer(이)가 1100년 영국 캄브리지실르 엘리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Bishop Humphrey of Salisbury Le Poer(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Ada Le Poer(은)는 15세였다. 그는 Maud Mistress of Neil Nigel Ramsbury(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1169년 5월 30일 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 엘리에서 69세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Bishop Neal "Nigel" of Ely Le Poer nasceu em 1100, em Ely, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Bishop Humphrey of Salisbury Le Poer, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Ada Le Poer, tinha 15 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Maud Mistress of Neil Nigel Ramsbury. Ele faleceu em 30 de maio de 1169, em Ely, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 69 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Bishop Neal "Nigel" of Ely Le Poer родился в 1100 г. в местности эли, камбриджешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Bishop Humphrey of Salisbury Le Poer было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Ada Le Poer, 15. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Maud Mistress of Neil Nigel Ramsbury. Он умер 30 мая 1169 г. в местности эли, камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 69 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Bishop Neal "Nigel" of Ely Le Poer nació en 1100, en Ely, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Bishop Humphrey of Salisbury Le Poer, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Ada Le Poer, tenía 15 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Maud Mistress of Neil Nigel Ramsbury. Murió el 30 de mayo de 1169, en Ely, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 69 años.
